title: rubus × asperrimus
intro: Rubus × asperrimus, also known as the hybrid blackberry, is a cross between the European blackberry (Rubus fruticosus) and the American dewberry (Rubus argutus), resulting in a plant with thorny canes and edible fruit. The medicinal properties of Rubus × asperrimus lie in its anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and protecting against certain diseases due to its high content of antioxidants and fiber. Rubus × asperrimus can be used to prepare herbal teas, jams, and jellies, as well as dried powders and extracts for medicinal purposes.
